channel.title: Per page discussions (new posts)
channel.description: Posts in the forum category "Per page discussions" - This category groups discussions related to particular pages within this site.

item.title: The Moloch Mentality: Re: Mind blown
item.content:encoded: Yeah we don't want to give each other ideas on this collaborative fiction website now do we.

item.title: The Moloch Mentality: Re: Mind blown
item.content:encoded: Well thank you! I won't hold my breath but it's a nice thought.

item.title: SCP-5197:
item.content:encoded:
I was hesitant to add more to the addendum because it's effectively a punchline, and I thought dragging it out would diminish the effect. Maybe I could have added more buildup, but I wasn't convinced that the anomaly was interesting enough that I could lengthen the article without losing readers' interest.

Anyways, I appreciate the feedback.

item.title: SCP-5109: +100
item.content:encoded:
+101 actually, but who's counting.

My first unambiguously successful SCP, the one that made me feel welcome on the site, the one that made me think I could actually write for this place. It was a great feeling then; it's a great feeling still. Thank you.

item.title: The Mekhanics of La Rue Part Two: Also known as: The Mekhanics Take Flight!
item.content:encoded:
Ahhh this has been a bit stressful writing it to make sure I didn't downgrade the characters or story! AM sorry for the wait!

Anyways on to the thanks! I would like to thank Cydhra, Mooagain, Vivarium, Mars, and Quadraginta for the critique and willingness to help me make sure this is as good as I can do with my… lack of writing techniques. I also wanna thank MalyceGraves and plaidypus for creating both the world of La Rue Macabre and the Mekhanics cause it is a lot of fun to write for the world.

And as always thank you for reading this, reader! I'm just happy to know your reading this.

(PS I am very sorry for the stiff prose, my skills are still terrible at writing in tale form)

item.title: The Moloch Mentality: Re: Mind blown
item.content:encoded: Don’t go around giving Harry ideas :P

item.title: SCP-5197:
item.content:encoded: I appreciate the dialogue between the O5 and the CSR guy, but I think this article could have been a lot more. Maybe sending the O5 further and further up the Customer Service chain would have been the cincher for me, but I'm not sure. The article is written well enough it's just missing something.

item.title: The Moloch Mentality: Mind blown
item.content:encoded: If the SCP brand ever hits the "big screen", this tale is movie material. +1

item.title: Dr. Michaels:
item.content:encoded: I likensome parts of this article a lot, but there are things I really didn't understand, and one of my pet peeves is authors who leave unexplained mysteries that they purposefully don't explain in the comments. No vote.

item.title: SCP-5197:
item.content:encoded: Definitely nice to read and I always enjoy some unknown group making contact with earth but I wish there were more details in the page pertaining to the SCP. I understand though the limited knowledge of the foundation which would cause this lack of more detailed info. Anyway really great not too long SCP. +1

item.title: SCP-5197: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I've been sitting on this draft for months now, so I think it's time I put this up.

Image provided by Dr Whitney and edited by DrAkimoto.

Thanks to Marcelles_Raynes and Sirslash47 for crit.

item.title: Document 507-3B:
item.content:encoded:
under 000-000-000?
"Requests: A wristwatch which makes an audible "tick" as the second hand moves. Both were approved."

I was wondering about that… someone should probably fix it right? As I understand it, that's just a simple grammar type error, but I'm new

item.title: SCP-5978:
item.content:encoded: I had to downvote because of the rampant grammar mistakes. However, a cheese grater that can grate literally anything does have potential (it could certainly be turned into an AO, at least) and could lead to some interesting cross-tests. Put this through SPaG checks and flesh out the idea a bit more, and you could have something special.

item.title: SCP-5127: Staff Post- Deletion Vote
item.content:encoded:
Beginning deletion vote at -11.

If you are not the author and you want to rewrite this article, you may reply to this post asking for the opportunity to do so. Please obtain permission from the author (or the Rewrite Team if this article is older than 6 months) and make sure you copy the page source to your sandbox. Please do not reply to this post for any other reason unless you are staff.

item.title: SCP-5266:
item.content:encoded: Hey, if you find a good cop in this world, you take 'em.

item.title: SCP-5057 Additional Documentation:
item.content:encoded:
A masterpiece. Nothing else, nothing more.

+1

item.title: Petition To Ban Margssentif:
item.content:encoded: holy shit

item.title: SCP-5127:
item.content:encoded:
If you aren't incredibly busy, I wouldn't mind your thoughts regarding the updated article.
Thank you!

item.title: SCP-5127:
item.content:encoded:
Ah beans, I did just write about something that can be locked up and forgotten about. Welp, back to the drawing board!
Thanks for your response!

item.title: SCP-5266:
item.content:encoded:
"and both subjects were offered positions in Foundation security after showing proficiency in responding to anomalous events and maintaining rational thought during a crisis."

Got my +1 in this moment. Love to see a caring foundation :)
